title: Bridge Essay: Abstract task representations for inference and control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Abstract, task, representations, for, inference.

**Abstract task representations for inference and control.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 35469725. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2022.03.009. Behavioral flexibility depends on our capacity to build and leverage abstract knowledge about tasks. Recently, two separate lines of research have implicated distinct brain networks in representing abstract task information: afrontoparietal cortical network, and anetwork involving the medial temporal lobe (MTL), medial prefrontal, and orbitofrontal cortex (OMPFC). These observations have mostly been made in parallel, with little attempt to understand their relationship. Here, we hypothesize th
